title: Judge Allows Aave to Move $71M in ETH from North Korea Hack
summary: A judge has permitted Aave to move $71 million in ETH stolen by North Korean hackers, but the funds remain legally frozen. This case highlights the challenges of recovering crypto stolen by state-sponsored actors.
body:
A New York judge has allowed the decentralized lending platform Aave to move $71 million in Ethereum (ETH) linked to a North Korean hack. The funds, currently frozen on the Arbitrum network, were stolen in a 2023 cyberattack. However, the legal freeze will follow the assets as plaintiffs continue their terrorism-related claim against the hackers.

This ruling is significant because it shows how crypto platforms can work with legal systems to recover stolen funds. While the ETH can now move to Aave, the legal case is ongoing, meaning the funds could still be seized or returned to victims. For regular users, this case underscores the risks of hacking in crypto and the importance of security measures.

If you hold crypto on platforms like Aave, this news is a reminder to stay vigilant. Keep an eye on security updates from your platform and consider using hardware wallets for large holdings. While recoveries like this are possible, prevention is always the best strategy.
twitterThread: 🚨 A judge just allowed Aave to move $71M in ETH stolen by North Korean hackers. But the funds are still legally frozen. Here's what this means for you.
In 2023, hackers linked to North Korea stole $71M in ETH. The funds were frozen on Arbitrum. Now, Aave can move them, but the legal case continues.
The part that matters: This shows how crypto platforms and courts can work together to recover stolen funds. But the case isn't over yet.
If you've ever worried about crypto hacks, this is a reminder: security is key. Platforms are getting better at recovering funds, but prevention is still the best approach.
Here's what you can do today: Enable two-factor authentication on your crypto accounts. Consider using a hardware wallet for large holdings.
standaloneTweet: A judge just allowed Aave to move $71M in ETH stolen by North Korean hackers. But the funds are still legally frozen. Here's the key takeaway: crypto platforms and courts are working together to recover stolen funds, but security is still your best defense. #CryptoSecurity
